cards.back: The rate at which data is transferred over a network

cards.front: Why is it necessary to vary the bandwidth?
cards.back: To accommodate different types of data and applications with varying data transfer rates
Dynamic bandwidth allocation, bandwidth prioritization, and QoS (Quality of Service) settings

cards.front: What is the purpose of varying the bandwidth on a network?
cards.back: To ensure efficient use of network resources, minimize congestion, and guarantee sufficient data transfer rates for critical applications

cards.front: What is the effect of varying the bandwidth on network performance?
cards.back: Improved network performance, reduced congestion, and better overall quality of service for all applications
